title: arenaria hispida subsp. guarensis
intro: Arenaria hispida subsp. guarensis, also known as the hairy sandwort, is a plant species that belongs to the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). It is native to the Iberian Peninsula and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems, due to its ability to soothe and calm irritated tissues. In herbal preparations, the roots of Arenaria hispida subsp. guarensis are often used to create infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be applied topically to benefit from its medicinal properties.
